subparts.text: What do you mean by Electric Flux density. 1
What do you mean by displacement current? 1
Define skin depth. Derive its relation with attenuation constant. 2
State stokes theorem along with its mathematical expression. 1
A lossy material has μ = 5μ₀, ε = 2ε₀. If at 5 MHz, the phase constant is 10 rad/m, calculate the loss tangent. 2
Give Maxwell’s equations in differential and integral form. 1

questions.question_text: State Divergence Theorem. Verify the divergence theorem for the function A = r2 ar + r sin θ cosφ aθ over the surface of a quarter of a hemisphere defined by 0 < r < 3, 0 < φ <π/2, 0 < θ < π/2 3
Given point P and vector A=y ax+ ay express P and A in cylindrical and spherical coordinates. Evaluate A at P in the Cartesian, cylindrical, and spherical systems. 2

Define electric flux density D and its relationship with the electric field intensity E and permittivity ϵ. State the differential form of Gauss's Law in terms of electric flux density. Explain its significance. 3
Derive the relationship between E and V. Also prove that tangential component of electric field undergoes no change on the boundary between two media. 3
Derive Ampere’s law. What are the characteristics of a static magnetic field? Discuss law of conservation of magnetic flux. Explain why magnetic monopole does not exist. 3
Discuss Biot Savart’s law. From it, derive magnetic field intensity H due to line, surface and volume current distributions. 2

Discuss any two applications of transmission lines. An air line has characteristic impedance of 70 Ω and phase constant of 3 rad/m at 100 MHz. Calculate the inductance per meter and the capacitance per meter of the line. 4
State Poynting theorem. Derive the mathematical expression for Poynting theorem. 2
